summary: Port the bash invoke_claude wrapper to Python with streaming NDJSON parsing and a Python mock_claude.py that is interchangeable with the existing bash mock. Provide a PATH shim so 'claude' resolves to the Python mock during tests on Windows and POSIX. Cover the wrapper and mock with layer-1 unit tests.

criteria.description: harness/lib/invoke.py exists, is valid Python, and exposes a callable invoke_claude(agent, prompt, max_turns=50, mcp_config=None).
testSteps: Import harness.lib.invoke
Assert invoke_claude is defined and callable
Inspect the signature: parameters agent, prompt, max_turns (default 50), mcp_config (default None)
criteria.threshold: module imports cleanly and signature matches

criteria.description: invoke_claude builds argv equivalent to the bash version: ['claude', '-p', PROMPT, '--agent', AGENT, '--max-turns', str(MAX), '--dangerously-skip-permissions', '--output-format', 'stream-json', '--verbose'].
testSteps: Patch subprocess.Popen to capture the argv passed to it
Call invoke_claude(agent='planner', prompt='hello', max_turns=10)
Assert all required flags appear in the captured argv in correct order/pairing
criteria.threshold: argv contains every required flag exactly once with correct values

criteria.description: When HARNESS_ROOT/.claude/settings.json exists, --settings <path> is appended to argv. When it does not exist, --settings is omitted.
testSteps: Set HARNESS_ROOT to a temp dir with .claude/settings.json present; capture argv; assert --settings <abs path> appears
Repeat with no settings.json; assert --settings is absent
criteria.threshold: both cases pass

criteria.description: When mcp_config is provided, --mcp-config <path> is appended to argv; when None, it is omitted.
testSteps: Capture argv with mcp_config='/tmp/mcp.json'; assert --mcp-config /tmp/mcp.json present
Capture argv with mcp_config=None; assert --mcp-config absent
criteria.threshold: both cases pass

criteria.description: invoke_claude resolves the claude executable via shutil.which (so a PATH-shimmed mock is found). When not found, it raises a clear FileNotFoundError or returns a non-zero error with a readable message mentioning 'claude'.
testSteps: Set PATH to an empty dir, call invoke_claude(...)
Assert a FileNotFoundError (or equivalent clear failure) is raised, with message referencing claude
criteria.threshold: raises descriptive error rather than crashing or hanging

criteria.description: invoke_claude returns the exit code from the subprocess unchanged (0 on success, non-zero on failure).
testSteps: Configure mock to exit 0; assert invoke_claude returns 0
Configure mock to exit 7; assert invoke_claude returns 7
criteria.threshold: exit code is propagated for at least two distinct values including 0 and a non-zero

criteria.description: invoke_claude reads the subprocess stdout line-by-line, parses each line as JSON, and tolerates malformed/non-JSON lines (skips them without raising).
testSteps: Feed a fake stdout pipe containing two valid NDJSON records and one malformed line
Assert invoke_claude completes successfully and the malformed line did not raise
criteria.threshold: no exception; valid records processed, invalid records skipped

criteria.description: When an NDJSON record of type 'assistant' contains a tool_use content block, invoke_claude writes a progress line to stderr that contains the tool name and a brief preview of the tool input.
testSteps: Capture stderr
Feed an NDJSON 'assistant' record with content [{type:'tool_use', name:'Bash', input:{command:'ls'}}]
Assert stderr contains the substring 'Bash' and 'ls'
criteria.threshold: tool name and preview appear in stderr output

criteria.description: When an NDJSON record of type 'result' contains total_cost_usd, a 'Cost: $<value>' line is written to stderr.
testSteps: Capture stderr
Feed an NDJSON 'result' record with total_cost_usd=0.0123
Assert stderr contains 'Cost:' and '0.0123'
criteria.threshold: cost line emitted to stderr

criteria.description: Progress output is written to stderr (not stdout), so callers piping stdout do not see decorations.
testSteps: Capture stdout and stderr separately
Run a scenario that emits a tool_use progress line
Assert the progress marker text appears only in stderr, not stdout
criteria.threshold: progress decorations confined to stderr

criteria.description: tests/helpers/mock_claude.py exists, has a Python shebang, runs as a standalone script, and reads the same env vars as the bash mock: MOCK_CLAUDE_FIXTURE_DIR (required), MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO (default 'pass'), MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG, MOCK_CLAUDE_STATE_DIR.
testSteps: Open tests/helpers/mock_claude.py and verify shebang line begins with '#!' and references python
Run 'python tests/helpers/mock_claude.py --agent planner -p test' without MOCK_CLAUDE_FIXTURE_DIR set; assert it errors out (parity with bash 'must be set' behavior)
Run with all env vars set; assert it exits 0
criteria.threshold: script exists, fails clearly on missing fixture dir, succeeds with full env

criteria.description: mock_claude.py routes by --agent value identically to the bash mock: 'planner' copies product-spec-minimal.md and sprint-plan-2sprint.json into harness-state/.
testSteps: Create temp cwd with harness-state/ dir
Set MOCK_CLAUDE_FIXTURE_DIR to fixtures/
Run mock_claude.py --agent planner -p 'Build a thing'
Assert harness-state/product-spec.md exists and equals fixtures/product-spec-minimal.md
Assert harness-state/sprint-plan.json exists and equals fixtures/sprint-plan-2sprint.json
criteria.threshold: both files copied; bytes match fixtures

criteria.description: mock_claude.py generator routing parity: when prompt mentions 'Propose contract' the contract-proposal-valid.json fixture is written to the right sprint dir; otherwise a status.json (ready-for-eval or blocked depending on MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO) plus generator-log.md are written.
testSteps: Run mock_claude.py --agent generator -p 'Propose contract for sprint-01'; assert contract-proposal.json fixture appears in harness-state/sprints/sprint-01/
Run mock_claude.py --agent generator -p 'Implement sprint-01'; assert status.json with status 'ready-for-eval' is written
Repeat with MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=fail-generator-blocked; assert status.json contains 'blocked'
criteria.threshold: all three routing branches behave identically to bash mock

criteria.description: mock_claude.py evaluator routing parity: 'review contract' writes contract-review.json (revise-then-accept scenario alternates revise then accepted across calls); 'Evaluate sprint' writes eval-report.json + status.json (pass on default scenario, fail on fail-eval scenario).
testSteps: Run with --agent evaluator -p 'review contract for sprint-01'; assert contract-review-accepted.json copied
Run with MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=revise-then-accept twice (first call -> contract-review-revise, second -> contract-review-accepted)
Run with --agent evaluator -p 'Evaluate sprint-01'; assert eval-report-pass.json copied and status.status == 'pass'
Run with MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=fail-eval -p 'Evaluate sprint-01'; assert eval-report-fail-blocking.json copied and status.status == 'fail'
criteria.threshold: all evaluator routing branches match bash mock

criteria.description: mock_claude.py logs each invocation to MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG with the same line format as the bash mock (call=N agent=NAME scenario=SCEN prompt=...) and maintains call-count and per-agent-count files in MOCK_CLAUDE_STATE_DIR.
testSteps: Run mock_claude.py three times with different agents
Assert MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG contains 3 lines each starting with 'call=' and including 'agent=' and 'scenario='
Assert state-dir/call-count contains '3'
Assert state-dir/<agent>-count files exist with correct counts
criteria.threshold: log format and state files match bash mock byte-for-byte where applicable

criteria.description: mock_claude.py emits a final usage_json line on stdout (a JSON object with session_id and usage.input_tokens / usage.output_tokens), matching the bash mock's terminal output.
testSteps: Capture stdout from a planner invocation
Assert stdout contains a parseable JSON object with session_id and usage fields
criteria.threshold: JSON usage record present on stdout

criteria.description: A PATH shim makes the Python mock invokable simply as 'claude'. On Windows, tests/helpers/claude.cmd execs python tests/helpers/mock_claude.py (forwarding all args). On POSIX, the existing bash 'claude' shim continues to work; if a Python-only POSIX shim is added it must be executable.
testSteps: Verify tests/helpers/claude.cmd exists on Windows and contains a python invocation of mock_claude.py with %* arg forwarding
Prepend tests/helpers/ to PATH; run shutil.which('claude'); assert a path is returned
Run subprocess.run(['claude', '--agent','planner','-p','hi'], env=...); assert exit code 0 and fixture files appear
criteria.threshold: claude resolves on PATH and behaves like the mock

criteria.description: The existing bash mock (tests/helpers/claude) MUST NOT be modified or deleted. It remains functional for bash users.
testSteps: git diff HEAD -- tests/helpers/claude shows no changes
tests/helpers/claude file still exists and is non-empty
criteria.threshold: bash mock untouched

criteria.description: No .sh, .bash, .ps1, or .bats files are modified in this sprint (per project-wide constraint). New .cmd shim is allowed.
testSteps: git diff --name-only origin/main...HEAD | filter for .sh, .ps1, .bats, .bash; assert empty
criteria.threshold: zero modified shell/bats/powershell files

criteria.description: tests/layer1/test_invoke.py exists and contains test methods for: argv construction (basic, with settings, with mcp_config), exit-code propagation, NDJSON parsing including malformed lines, tool_use progress to stderr, cost line to stderr, claude-not-found behavior, and PATH shim resolution.
testSteps: Open tests/layer1/test_invoke.py
Assert at least 8 test methods (def test_*) exist covering the categories listed
criteria.threshold: >= 8 test methods present, covering each listed category at least once

criteria.description: Running 'python tests/run-all.py layer1' from project root passes all tests, including the new test_invoke tests and the prior test_utils tests (no regressions).
testSteps: From project root, run: python tests/run-all.py layer1
Assert exit code 0
Assert output reports OK (or equivalent) and includes test_invoke and test_utils suites
criteria.threshold: exit code 0, all tests pass

criteria.description: Sprint 1 deliverables continue to function: harness/lib/utils.py imports cleanly, tests/layer1/test_utils.py still passes (no regressions introduced).
testSteps: python -c 'from harness.lib import utils; print(utils.slugify("Hello World"))'; assert exit 0 and output 'hello-world'
python -m unittest tests.layer1.test_utils -v; assert exit code 0
criteria.threshold: all sprint-1 tests still pass

criteria.description: harness/lib/invoke.py and tests/helpers/mock_claude.py use only Python 3.8+ standard library modules (no third-party imports).
testSteps: Inspect import statements in both files
Assert every imported top-level package is in the stdlib whitelist (json, os, sys, subprocess, shutil, pathlib, re, datetime, typing, argparse, etc.)
criteria.threshold: no third-party imports

criteria.description: The Python mock and the bash mock produce equivalent harness-state artifacts for the planner agent invocation (same files, same content). On Windows-without-bash, this test skips with a clear message.
testSteps: In two separate temp dirs with the same env vars, run the bash mock (where bash is available) and the Python mock with --agent planner
Diff harness-state/product-spec.md and harness-state/sprint-plan.json between the two dirs
Assert both files are byte-identical
criteria.threshold: byte-identical fixtures produced; skips cleanly when bash unavailable

criteria.description: Sprint work is committed with messages following the harness convention referencing sprint-02 and at least one criterion id.
testSteps: git log --oneline; assert at least one commit message starts with 'harness(sprint-02):' and references a [C2-XX] id
criteria.threshold: >= 1 conformant commit

files.purpose: Python port of invoke.sh: subprocess + streaming NDJSON + progress display
Python port of bash mock claude with same env-var contract and fixture routing
Windows PATH shim that execs python tests/helpers/mock_claude.py
Unit tests for invoke.py and mock plumbing
Optional: ensure layer1 runner picks up test_invoke (likely already auto-discovers)
Optional: add helper for prepending tests/helpers to PATH or counting mock invocations
Add sprint 2 to completedSprints with new key files

outOfScope: Modifying harness/lib/invoke.sh, tests/helpers/claude, or any .sh/.ps1/.bats/.bash file
Adding third-party Python packages or pyproject.toml/requirements.txt
